questions.text: Which one is not related to evaporation?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This term refers to a process that happens without interruption. Evaporation can be continuous, but it is not a defining characteristic of the process itself.
Evaporation is indeed an endothermic process, meaning it absorbs heat from the surroundings. This is a key feature of evaporation.
This term describes a process that releases heat. Since evaporation absorbs heat, it is not exothermic, making it the correct answer to the question.

This term means that a process can occur on its own without external influence. Evaporation can be spontaneous under certain conditions, but it is not inherently linked to the definition of evaporation.

questions.explanation: Evaporation is a continuous and spontaneous process. It requires absorption of heat from surroundings, so it is endothermic. Therefore, exothermic is not related to evaporation

questions.text: Water has maximum density at:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: At -4 C, water would be in a solid state (ice), which is less dense than liquid water.
At -1 C, water is close to freezing, and its density decreases as it approaches the solid state.
At 0 C, water begins to form ice, which is less dense due to the crystal structure of ice.
At 4 C, water reaches its maximum density because the water molecules are most closely packed.

questions.explanation: Water exhibits an anomalous behavior where its density increases as it cools until it reaches 4 C, where it is most dense. Below 4 C, water begins to expand again as it approaches the freezing point, due to the formation of a crystalline structure in ice. Therefore, the maximum density of water occurs at 4 C. The other options represent temperatures where water is either starting to freeze (-1 C, 0 C) or is already frozen (-4 C), both of which result in a lower density than at 4 C.

questions.text: An example of a solution not in equilibrium is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Incorrect. A chemical pH indicator changes color based on the pH of the solution as it reaches different equilibria states. The indicator itself doesn't disturb the equilibrium of the solution it's in.
Incorrect. An acid-base buffer is designed to maintain equilibrium by neutralizing added acids or bases to resist pH changes, effectively maintaining a stable equilibrium condition.
Incorrect. An anhydrous solution simply lacks water. Whether it is in equilibrium depends on the components and conditions, and being anhydrous does not inherently mean it is out of equilibrium.

Correct. A hypotonic solution can lead to water movement across semipermeable membranes, such as into cells, causing an osmotic imbalance. This movement indicates that the system is initially not in equilibrium until the water movement ceases.

questions.explanation: A hypotonic solution can cause water to move across a semipermeable membrane (like into a cell), creating an osmotic imbalance. This movement continues until equilibrium is reached, so initially, it is not in equilibrium

questions.text: Upon which factor is vapor pressure not dependent:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The greater the temperature, the more molecules will gain sufficient K.E and hence, the greater the vapor pressure. Thus, the V.P of a liquid is dependent on the temperature.

The stronger the intermolecular forces, the lesser will be the V.P as the molecules will be tightly bound to each other and won’t be able to escape easily. Hence, the V.P is also inversely dependent on the intermolecular forces.
As the density increases the pressure also increases due to a decrease in the volume of the liquid. Hence, V.P is also dependent on the density of the liquid.

The V.P. of a liquid is independent of the surface area of the liquid. This is because the rate of evaporation is balanced with the rate of condensation. And any increase in the surface area will not be sufficient to increase the V.P until the molecules don’t have sufficient energy of action. Hence, this is the correct option.

questions.explanation: The surface area of liquid or solid in contact does not affect vapor pressure. Other factors do have an impact on vapor pressure. Higher density decreases vapor pressure, higher temperature increases vapor pressure and higher intermolecular forces also decrease the vapor pressure.

questions.text: Which one of the following has the highest lattice energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The lattice enthalpy is the enthalpy change involved in the formation of an ionic compound from gaseous ions (an exothermic process).The Lattice energy is directly proportional to the charges of the ions but inversely proportional to the radii of the ions or more generally the distance between ions. Lattice Energy α (charge of ion/radius of the ion).As we go down in a group (Group 1 and 7 in this case), ionic radius increases, and as the net charge is constant (+1 and -1 respectively), the lattice energy of their compounds decreases.Order of cation radius: K+> Na+> Li+Order of anion radius: Br-> Cl-Hence, the compound LiCl will have the greatest Lattice energy as both the ions have the least radius.Note: Across a period from left to right as the charge on ion increases, lattice energy increases.

The lattice enthalpy is the enthalpy change involved in the formation of an ionic compound from gaseous ions (an exothermic process).The Lattice energy is directly proportional to the charges of the ions but inversely proportional to the radii of the ions or more generally the distance between ions. Lattice Energy α (charge of ion/radius of the ion).As we go down in a group (Group 1 and 7 in this case), ionic radius increases, and as the net charge is constant (+1 and -1 respectively), the lattice energy of their compounds decreases.Order of cation radius: K+> Na+> Li+Order of anion radius: Br-> Cl-Hence, the compound LiCl will have the greatest Lattice energy as both the ions have the least radius.Note: Across a period from left to right as the charge on ion increases, lattice energy increases.

The lattice enthalpy is the enthalpy change involved in the formation of an ionic compound from gaseous ions (an exothermic process).The Lattice energy is directly proportional to the charges of the ions but inversely proportional to the radii of the ions or more generally the distance between ions. Lattice Energy α (charge of ion/radius of the ion).As we go down in a group (Group 1 and 7 in this case), ionic radius increases, and as the net charge is constant (+1 and -1 respectively), the lattice energy of their compounds decreases.Order of cation radius: K+> Na+> Li+Order of anion radius: Br-> Cl-Hence, the compound LiCl will have the greatest Lattice energy as both the ions have the least radius.Note: Across a period from left to right as the charge on ion increases, lattice energy increases.

The lattice enthalpy is the enthalpy change involved in the formation of an ionic compound from gaseous ions (an exothermic process).The Lattice energy is directly proportional to the charges of the ions but inversely proportional to the radii of the ions or more generally the distance between ions. Lattice Energy α (charge of ion/radius of the ion).As we go down in a group (Group 1 and 7 in this case), ionic radius increases, and as the net charge is constant (+1 and -1 respectively), the lattice energy of their compounds decreases.Order of cation radius: K+> Na+> Li+Order of anion radius: Br-> Cl-Hence, the compound LiCl will have the greatest Lattice energy as both the ions have the least radius.Note: Across a period from left to right as the charge on ion increases, lattice energy increases.

questions.explanation: The lattice enthalpy is the enthalpy change involved in the formation of an ionic compound from gaseous ions (an exothermic process).The Lattice energy is directly proportional to the charges of the ions but inversely proportional to the radii of the ions or more generally the distance between ions. Lattice Energy α (charge of ion/radius of the ion).As we go down in a group (Group 1 and 7 in this case), ionic radius increases, and as the net charge is constant (+1 and -1 respectively), the lattice energy of their compounds decreases.Order of cation radius: K+> Na+> Li+Order of anion radius: Br-> Cl-Hence, the compound LiCl will have the greatest Lattice energy as both the ions have the least radius.Note: Across a period from left to right as the charge on ion increases, lattice energy increases.

questions.text: London forces are present in
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ammonia exhibits London forces, along with hydrogen bonding due to its polar nature.
Water has London forces, but its more prominent intermolecular force is hydrogen bonding.
London forces are present in kerosene. Kerosene is a mixture of hydrocarbons, and the predominant forces holding its molecules together are van der Waals forces.
HCl exhibits dipole-dipole interactions, which are stronger than London forces. However, London forces are still present in HCl due to the temporary fluctuations in electron distribution.

questions.explanation: London forces are present in kerosene oil. The London dispersion force is caused by random and temporary changes in the polarity of atoms, caused by the location of the electrons in the atoms' orbitals.

questions.text: Which of the following shows H-bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This molecule has a hydrogen atom bonded to an oxygen atom, which can participate in hydrogen bonding interactions. Hydrogen bonding typically occurs when hydrogen is directly bonded to a highly electronegative atom (such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine), and there is a lone pair of electrons on the electronegative atom.

This molecule has oxygen, but the hydrogen atoms are not directly bonded to oxygen. Hydrogen bonding requires a hydrogen atom directly bonded to a highly electronegative atom (like oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine) with a lone pair of electrons on that electronegative atom.

This molecule contains chlorine, but hydrogen bonding requires a hydrogen atom directly bonded to an electronegative atom with a lone pair of electrons. Chlorine is not as electronegative as oxygen or nitrogen, and there is no hydrogen directly bonded to it with a lone pair.
This option is incorrect because only option (a) CH₃CH₂OH (ethanol) shows hydrogen bonding. Options (b) and (c) do not exhibit hydrogen bonding.

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding in alcohols. Alcohol is an organic molecule containing an -O-H group. Any molecule that has a hydrogen atom attached directly to an oxygen or nitrogen is capable of hydrogen bonding.

questions.text: The properties of liquid crystals are intermediate between crystals and:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Anisotropic liquids are liquids that exhibit different physical properties in different directions in space. This is because of the random arrangement of particles. Hence, this option is the incorrect one.

The nematic liquid is a type of anisotropic liquid that lacks positional order; its atoms are randomly arranged. They have elongated molecules, but not all liquid crystals are nematic. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Correct. Isotropic liquids have no long-range order and uniform properties in all directions. Liquid crystals have more order than isotropic liquids but less than solid crystals, making them intermediate between crystals and isotropic liquids.
Incorrect. This is too general. The proper comparison is to isotropic liquids, which lack directional order, unlike liquid crystals.

questions.explanation: Correct. Isotropic liquids have no long-range order and uniform properties in all directions. Liquid crystals have more order than isotropic liquids but less than solid crystals, making them intermediate between crystals and isotropic liquids.

questions.text: The liquid in the container has a temperature of 70°C. What will be the temperature on the Kelvin scale?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: To convert temperature from celsius to kelvin scale the formula is°C + 273.15 K 70° + 273.15 =343 K.
To convert temperature from celsius to kelvin scale the formula is°C + 273.15 K 70° + 273.15 =343 K.
According to the solution, this is not the correct option.To convert temperature from celsius to kelvin scale the formula is°C + 273.15 K 70° + 273.15 =343 K.
According to the solution, this is not the correct option. To convert temperature from celsius to kelvin scale the formula is°C + 273.15 K 70° + 273.15 =343 K.

questions.explanation: To convert temperature from celsius to kelvin scale the formula is°C + 273.15 K 70° + 273.15 =343 K.

questions.text: The rate of diffusion of liquids is _ than gases.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The rate of diffusion of liquids is lower compared to gases. In the process of diffusion, particles move from an area of higher concentration to an area of lower concentration. Gases have greater kinetic energy and are more widely spaced compared to liquids, allowing gas particles to move more freely and quickly. As a result, gases diffuse at a faster rate than liquids.
This option is incorrect. As explained above, the rate of diffusion of gases is higher than that of liquids.
This option is incorrect. The rate of diffusion of liquids is not equal to that of gases. Gases diffuse faster due to their higher kinetic energy and more significant intermolecular spaces.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Only option "a) Lower" is correct.

questions.explanation: Diffusion is the intermixing (movement) of molecules. When looking at diffusion you must look at two factors: forces of attraction and kinetic energy of molecules. Liquids have a stronger force of attraction than gases, and liquids have less KE than gases. This is because gas particles are further apart compared to liquid particles and move faster. This makes liquids have a lower rate of diffusion than gases.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest boiling point?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Aldehydes and Ketones

options.explanation: Propanal is an aldehyde with the molecular formula C3H6O. It has polar carbonyl groups that create dipole-dipole interactions; however, it cannot form hydrogen bonds. This results in a relatively lower boiling point compared to alcohols that can engage in stronger hydrogen bonding.

Propan-1-ol is a three-carbon alcohol with the molecular formula C3H8O. Its -OH group allows it to form strong hydrogen bonds, which significantly increases its boiling point compared to other compounds listed that rely on weaker intermolecular forces. This makes it the compound with the highest boiling point among the options.

Propane is a three-carbon alkane with the molecular formula C3H8. It primarily exhibits weak dispersion forces, which result in a much lower boiling point compared to the other options that can form stronger intermolecular interactions.

Ethoxyethane, also known as diethyl ether, is an ether with the molecular formula C4H10O. Although it has some polar characteristics due to the ether oxygen, it predominantly relies on dipole-dipole interactions and cannot form hydrogen bonds like propan-1-ol. This results in a lower boiling point compared to propan-1-ol.

questions.explanation: Propan-1-ol can form strong intermolecular hydrogen bonds due to its -OH group. These are much stronger than the dipole-dipole forces in propanal and ethoxyethane, and the weak dispersion forces in propane, giving it the highest boiling point.

questions.text: Which of the following exerts the lowest vapour pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Chloroform has a lower vapor pressure compared to the other options. It has relatively strong intermolecular forces, so it doesn't easily evaporate at room temperature.
Methanol has a higher vapor pressure compared to chloroform. It has weaker intermolecular forces, allowing it to evaporate more readily
Water has a lower vapor pressure compared to methanol and chloroform. It has relatively strong intermolecular forces, which make it less likely to evaporate quickly.
Ethanol has a higher vapor pressure compared to water but lower than methanol. It has weaker intermolecular forces, allowing it to evaporate more easily than water.

questions.explanation: The stronger the intermolecular forces, the lower the vapor pressure. Water has the strongest forces amongst these options (4 H-bonds), hence it will have the lowest vapor pressure.

questions.text: At 0°C what is the physical state of water?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Below 0 degree C, water exists in the form of ice
Between 0 degree and 100 degree C, water exists in the form of liquid
Above 100 degrees C , water exists in the form of vapours
Both water and ice exist in this phase of equilibrium at 0 degree C. Ice and water are in equilibrium at this temperature.

questions.explanation: 0 degree C at 1 atm pressure is the freezing point of pure water where its solid-state and liquid state coexist in an equilibrium.

questions.text: Liquids have _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. All states of matter, including solids, liquids, and gases, have a definite mass.
This option is incorrect. Liquids do not have a definite shape; they take the shape of their container.
This option is correct. Liquids have a definite volume but not a definite shape. They conform to the shape of their container.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because option C is indeed correct. Liquids have a definite volume but not a definite shape.

questions.explanation: Liquids have a definite volume but do not have a definite shape; they take on the shape of the container they are in. This is in contrast to gases, which neither have a definite shape nor a definite volume, and solids, which have both a definite shape and volume. Therefore, the correct answer is that liquids have a definite volume.

questions.text: Cooling is caused by _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: a) Vapour pressure:Vapour pressure is the pressure exerted by the vapor of a substance in equilibrium with its liquid phase at a given temperature. While vapour pressure plays a role in various physical processes, it is not directly responsible for cooling.
Evaporation produces cooling as the particles at the surface of the liquid gain energy from the surroundings and change into vapour, thereby producing a cooling effect.
c) Freezing:Freezing is the process of a liquid turning into a solid as it loses heat. While freezing involves the release of latent heat, it is not a process that causes cooling.

options.text: None of these options are correct
options.explanation: d) None of these options are correct:The correct option for cooling is b) Evaporation, as explained above.

questions.explanation: Evaporation produces cooling as the particles at the surface of the liquid gain energy from the surroundings and change into vapour, thereby producing a cooling effect.

questions.text: How many times is a covalent bond stronger than a hydrogen bond?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: If we compare the strength of covalent bonds to hydrogen bonds, covalent bonds are much stronger. A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons, creating a strong bond. On the other hand, a hydrogen bond is a relatively weak bond that occurs when a hydrogen atom is attracted to an electronegative atom (like oxygen or nitrogen) in another molecule. So, a covalent bond is significantly stronger than a hydrogen bond.

If we compare the strength of covalent bonds to hydrogen bonds, covalent bonds are much stronger. A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons, creating a strong bond. On the other hand, a hydrogen bond is a relatively weak bond that occurs when a hydrogen atom is attracted to an electronegative atom (like oxygen or nitrogen) in another molecule. So, a covalent bond is significantly stronger than a hydrogen bond.

The strength of a covalent bond is approximately 20 times stronger than a hydrogen bond because of the difference in bond energies. Let's break this down:Covalent Bond StrengthCovalent bonds involve the sharing of electron pairs between atoms.Their bond energy typically ranges from 200 to 400 kJ/mol (for common bonds like C-H, O-H, etc.).Hydrogen Bond StrengthHydrogen bonds are weaker interactions between a hydrogen atom (attached to an electronegative atom like O or N) and another electronegative atom.Their bond energy typically ranges from 10 to 20 kJ/mol.ComparisonIf we take an average covalent bond energy of 400 kJ/mol and a hydrogen bond energy of 20 kJ/mol, the ratio is:400/20 = 20This means a covalent bond is about 20 times stronger than a hydrogen bond.

If we compare the strength of covalent bonds to hydrogen bonds, covalent bonds are much stronger. A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons, creating a strong bond. On the other hand, a hydrogen bond is a relatively weak bond that occurs when a hydrogen atom is attracted to an electronegative atom (like oxygen or nitrogen) in another molecule. So, a covalent bond is significantly stronger than a hydrogen bond.

questions.explanation: If we compare the strength of covalent bonds to hydrogen bonds, covalent bonds are much stronger. A covalent bond is formed when two atoms share electrons, creating a strong bond. On the other hand, a hydrogen bond is a relatively weak bond that occurs when a hydrogen atom is attracted to an electronegative atom (like oxygen or nitrogen) in another molecule. So, a covalent bond is significantly stronger than a hydrogen bond.

questions.text: K.E of the liquids is directly proportional to _?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Pressure affects the boiling points of liquids, but it is not directly proportional to the kinetic energy of the molecules.
When the temperature of molecules increases, they gain energy and move and collide more, hence kinetic energy of the liquids is directly proportional to the temperature.
The mass of the liquid does not affect the kinetic energy of the molecules.

The nature of the liquid, which refers to its chemical composition and structure, can affect its boiling point, surface tension, and other physical properties, but it is not directly proportional to the kinetic energy of the molecules.

questions.explanation: When the temperature of molecules increases, they gain energy and move and collide more, hence temperature is directly proportional to temperature. The energy they gain is kinetic energy.

questions.text: Adhesive nature of honey and glue is due to the presence of?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is a type of intermolecular force that occurs between a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly Electronegative element (such as oxygen or nitrogen) and another Electronegative element in a nearby molecule. Hydrogen bonding is a stronger intermolecular force and is responsible for the adhesive nature of honey and glue.

Dipole-dipole forces are another type of intermolecular force that occurs between polar molecules. However, these forces are weaker than Hydrogen bonding and are not the primary reason for the adhesive nature of honey and glue.

Ionic forces are electrostatic forces that occur between oppositely charged ions. While Ionic forces can be strong, they are not the primary type of force responsible for the adhesive nature of honey and glue.

Debye forces, also known as London dispersion forces, are weak intermolecular forces that occur between all molecules, regardless of their polarity. However, these forces are not strong enough to be the primary reason for the adhesive nature of honey and glue.

questions.explanation: Glue is made of PVA and water molecules. The oxygen in water molecules has lone pairs that can form hydrogen bonds with the h of another molecule. Honey is made of glucose and fructose hence h- bonds form between H of the glucose and fructose and the lone pairs of oxygen.

questions.text: In non-polar molecules ,the strength of London forces depends on number of?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The number of moles does not directly affect the strength of London forces. London forces are intermolecular forces that occur between individual molecules.

The strength of London forces in non-polar molecules does not depend on the number of molecules because these forces arise due to the temporary fluctuations in electron density within individual molecules.

The strength of London forces is directly proportional to the number of atoms present in a molecule. Larger molecules with more atoms have more electrons, which creates a larger electron cloud and increases the likelihood of temporary dipoles forming.
this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: In nonpolar molecules, there are induced dipole-dipole forces and its strength is dependent on the number of electrons hence the number of atoms. Greater the number of electrons stronger the dipole-dipole force.

questions.text: The boiling point of water is higher than that of HF because of _ hydrogen bonding.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Both H2O and HF have electronegative oxygen and fluorine atoms respectively in their molecule. In fact, Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, so technically, HF should engage in more hydrogen bonding than water. But the scenario is different. Each water molecule has two hydrogen atoms. Whereas, each HF molecule has only one hydrogen atom. And hence, water molecule takes part in extensive and more hydrogen bonding than Hydrofluoric acid. Water molecule through its extensive hydrogen bonding forms bulky molecule and it is very difficult to break its bonds. A large amount of energy is required to break all its bonds. Thus H20 has a higher boiling point than HF.

False!Both H2O and HF have electronegative oxygen and fluorine atoms respectively in their molecule. In fact, Fluorine is more electronegative than Oxygen, so technically, HF should engage in more hydrogen bonding than water. But the scenario is different. Each water molecule has two hydrogen atoms. questions.text: Which of the following would not mix with each other easily?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: They will mix together easily, as they have hydrogen bonding between them.
They will mix together easily, as they have hydrogen bonding between them.

Pentane is an organic compound that is non- polar and water is a polar molecule, hence they won't mix together because water cannot form hydrogen bonds with pentane, hence the correct answer is Option C.
They will mix together easily, as they have hydrogen bonding between them.

questions.explanation: Pentane is an organic compound that is non-polar and water is a polar molecule, hence they won't mix together because water cannot form hydrogen bonds with pentane, hence the correct answer is Option C.

questions.text: Space among the liquids is _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Liquids are more dense and have less space between particles than gases.

Solids are the dense state of matter, with particles packed closely together in a regular pattern, so there is little space between them. Therefore, the space between liquids is between solids and gases, but higher than solids.
This option is incorrect. The space among the liquids is higher than solids, not lower than solids.

When a solid changes into a liquid the atoms inside experience two variations. First, the distance between particles increases, and therefore the force of attraction between them decreases. Increasing distance means that now particles cover more space than they were covering in a solid. But when a liquid changes into gas, the particle's distance increases more than in liquid, and the force of attraction between them decreases. Therefore, the space among the liquids is lower than gases and higher than solids.

questions.explanation: When a solid changes into a liquid the atoms inside experience two variations. First, the distance between particles increases, and therefore the force of attraction between them decreases. Increasing distance means that now particles cover more space than they were covering in a solid. But when a liquid changes into gas, the particle's distance increases more than in liquid, and the force of attraction between them decreases. So therefore a suitable answer will be D; lower than gases and higher than solids.

questions.text: What is the relationship between boiling point and External pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: Boiling point increases if external pressure is increased
options.explanation: The boiling point is the temperature when the internal/vapor pressure of the liquid becomes equal to the external/atmospheric pressure. If external pressure increases, the vapor pressure must increase as well and become equal to the external pressure for the liquid to boil. As temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point increases if external pressure decreases
options.explanation: It is incorrect because as temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point is not affected by external pressure
options.explanation: It is incorrect because as temperature increases the vapor pressure increases and vice versa.

options.text: Boiling point decreases if external pressure increased

questions.text: Which of the following has more evaporation rate at the same temperature?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: A higher evaporation rate ( higher volatility) means that intermolecular forces are weaker and hence are easily overcome leading to the molecules escaping from the surface of the liquid. Gasoline is a mixture of volatile, flammable hydrocarbons. Hydrocarbons have weak instantaneous dipole-induced dipole forces between them. Water and Alcohol both have strong intermolecular forces: hydrogen bonding, which requires more energy to overcome. Honey being very viscous/ having higher density has the strongest intermolecular forces of attraction ( greater number of hydrogen bonds).

Water has stronger intermolecular forces due to hydrogen bonding, and its molecular weight is higher compared to gasoline. While water can evaporate, it tends to evaporate more slowly than substances with weaker intermolecular forces.
Honey is a highly viscous liquid with strong intermolecular forces, and its evaporation rate is generally much slower than that of liquids with weaker forces.
Ethanol has a lower molecular weight compared to water and has weaker intermolecular forces. Therefore, ethanol is likely to have a higher evaporation rate than water.

The boiling point is directly proportional to IMF i.e. BP of water>ethylalcohol.

questions.explanation: The boiling point of a liquid doesn’t depend on the amount of liquid or the surface area of a liquid. It does depend on the intermolecular forces, vapor pressure, and external pressure. The intermolecular forces determine the amount of energy needed to overcome bonds. When a liquid is heated, the vapor pressure increases. At one point, the vapor pressure reaches the external pressure. This is the boiling point.

questions.text: The strength of London forces depends on size of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since the size of all electrons is the same.

Option B is correct since the London dispersion force is a temporary attractive force that results when the electrons in two adjacent atoms occupy positions that make the atoms form temporary dipoles. The greater the size of the electron cloud, the greater will be the asymmetrical distribution of electrons and the greater will be the tendency of a molecule to be charged.

questions.text: Due to which of the following forces present, is aquatic life protected in cold climate?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Dipole-dipole forces do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

Debye forces, also known as London dispersion forces, do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

Hydrogen bonding is a type of intermolecular force that occurs between a hydrogen atom bonded to an electronegative atom (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom in a different molecule. In the case of antifreeze proteins, these proteins contain regions that are rich in amino acids that can form hydrogen bonds with ice crystals. When the protein binds to the surface of an ice crystal, it prevents further growth of the crystal by inhibiting the addition of more water molecules to the crystal lattice. This allows the organism to survive in sub-zero temperatures by preventing ice from forming inside its cells and causing damage to its tissues.

London dispersion forces, also known as Van der Waals forces, do not provide significant protection for aquatic life in cold climates. While these forces can help stabilize the orientation of water molecules and prevent ice formation to some extent, they are not as effective as hydrogen bonding in preventing ice crystal growth and protecting organisms from sub-zero temperatures.

questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is a type of intermolecular force that occurs between a hydrogen atom bonded to an electronegative atom (such as nitrogen, oxygen, or fluorine) and another electronegative atom in a different molecule. In the case of antifreeze proteins, these proteins contain regions that are rich in amino acids that can form hydrogen bonds with ice crystals. When the protein binds to the surface of an ice crystal, it prevents further growth of the crystal by inhibiting the addition of more water molecules to the crystal lattice. This allows the organism to survive in sub-zero temperatures by preventing ice from forming inside its cells and causing damage to its tissues.
hints: aquatic life protected in cold climate

questions.text: Which of the following has least polarizability?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: NH3 (ammonia) has polarizability. While ammonia is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

H2O (water) has polarizability. Water is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, but it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

HF (hydrogen fluoride) has polarizability. While HF is a polar molecule with a net dipole moment, it is still capable of being polarized by an external electric field due to its electron cloud distribution. This means that it has non-zero polarizability.

Methane (CH4) has the least polarizability of these molecules because it is a nonpolar molecule. This means that it has no permanent dipole moment and its electron density is distributed evenly around the molecule. Since polarizability is a measure of how easily the electron cloud of a molecule can be distorted by an electric field, nonpolar molecules like methane have very low polarizabilities. In contrast, polar molecules with large dipole moments have higher polarizabilities because their electron clouds are more easily distorted by external electric fields.

questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) has the least polarizability of these molecules because it is a nonpolar molecule. This means that it has no permanent dipole moment and its electron density is distributed evenly around the molecule. Since polarizability is a measure of how easily the electron cloud of a molecule can be distorted by an electric field, nonpolar molecules like methane have very low polarizabilities. In contrast, polar molecules with large dipole moments have higher polarizabilities because their electron clouds are more easily distorted by external electric fields.

Water has a higher boiling point because the molecule is held together by strong hydrogen bonding which is between the partial positive charge on hydrogen and partial negative charge on oxygen therefore more heat is required to break its bond thus a higher boiling point.

HF (hydrogen fluoride) has stronger intermolecular forces compared to NH3 but is still lower than water. The presence of hydrogen bonding in HF contributes to its higher boiling point compared to NH3.
CH4 (methane) has only London dispersion forces, which are the weakest intermolecular forces. As a result, CH4 has the lowest boiling point among the given options.

questions.explanation: Water has a higher boiling point because the molecule is held together by strong hydrogen bonding which is between the partial positive charge on hydrogen and partial negative charge on oxygen therefore more heat is required to break its bond thus a higher boiling point.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about the boiling point of water in a pressure cooker?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.text: It is higher than the normal boiling point
options.explanation: The boiling point of water in a pressure cooker is higher than the normal boiling point. A pressure cooker is a sealed container that allows steam to build up inside it, increasing the pressure and raising the boiling point of water. This increase in pressure raises the boiling point of water above its normal boiling point of 100°C (at standard atmospheric pressure). This higher temperature in the pressure cooker can reduce cooking times for certain foods and can also help to tenderize tougher cuts of meat.

options.text: It is lower than the normal boiling point
options.explanation: The boiling point of water in a pressure cooker is higher than the normal boiling point. A pressure cooker is a sealed container that allows steam to build up inside it, increasing the pressure and raising the boiling point of water. This increase in pressure raises the boiling point of water above its normal boiling point of 100°C (at standard atmospheric pressure). This higher temperature in the pressure cooker can reduce cooking times for certain foods and can also help to tenderize tougher cuts of meat.

options.text: It is lower than the freezing point
options.explanation: This statement is not related to the boiling point of water in a pressure cooker. The boiling point and freezing point are different properties of a substance. The freezing point is the temperature at which a liquid changes to a solid, whereas the boiling point is the temperature at which a liquid changes to a gas.

This option is not correct because only option (a) is true. The boiling point of water in a pressure cooker is higher than the normal boiling point, but it is not lower than the normal boiling point or lower than the freezing point.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct since a pressure cooker is based on the principle that the boiling point of water increases with the increase in pressure. It works by capturing the steam that, as it builds up, increases the pressure in the vessel. The pressure increase in turn raises the boiling point of water, which normally limits the cooking temperature of wet foods to 100°C. Because the effective cooking temperature is higher in the pressure cooker—as high as 120°C —the cooking time can drop substantially.

questions.explanation: In the passage queen Elizabeth has a II with it, this shows that there has been another queen with Elizabeth in her name, Brexit is not a normal occurrence and the passage clearly states that she rules post world war 2 so only statement 1 is correct.Based on the passage, the correct option is:I. There has been another queen of the British throne named Elizabeth before her.The passage refers to Queen Elizabeth II, indicating that there was a Queen Elizabeth I before her.The passage does not support statements II and III:- II. Brexit is not described as a normal occurrence, but rather as one of the cultural upheavals that Queen Elizabeth II witnessed during her reign.- III. The passage states that Queen Elizabeth II's reign spanned "virtually the entire post-World War II era", implying that she was not queen during World War II itself.

questions.text: Statement:According to reports, the childrens below age 10 are being aggressive because of mobile phones.Course of Action:I. Parents needs to be strict with their child.II. Parents need to be concerned and limit their screening time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.topic: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Both of the courses of action follow
options.explanation: this is not a correct option. The root cause of children getting aggressive is their mobile phones hence limiting the time that children use their phones can solve this problem. Lenient parentage is not the problem plus strict parenting sometimes causes rebellious behavior.

options.text: None of the courses of action follow
